---
title: "About"
page-layout: article
---

## The Lab Philosophy

**The Modeled World** is where I apply the Feynman Technique to data science: learning by teaching, teaching by doing, doing by showing the code.

This isn't a polished portfolio site. It's a lab notebook. You'll see experiments, failures, and thought processes - not just final results.

## What I Write About

- **Sports Analytics**: Betting models, player props, performance predictions
- **Economic Trends**: Inflation tracking, CPI analysis, macro indicators
- **Code Experiments**: API integrations, Python tutorials, MLOps workflows

Every post includes reproducible code, real data sources, and interactive visualizations.

## The Stack

- **Language**: Python (Pandas, Plotly, scikit-learn)
- **Tools**: Jupyter, Quarto, VS Code
- **Publishing**: GitHub Pages, automated via GitHub Actions
- **Data Sources**: College Football Data API, NBA API, FRED, odds providers

## Why This Exists

I'm a Senior Data Scientist who learns best by building in public. If these experiments help you learn Python, understand sports analytics, or track economic trends - that's the goal.

## Contact

- **Medium**: [@ben.g.ballard](https://medium.com/@ben.g.ballard)
- **LinkedIn**: [Your LinkedIn URL]
- **GitHub**: [Your GitHub username]

Or just drop a comment on any post.

---

## Disclaimer

Nothing here is financial advice. Sports betting analysis is for educational purposes. Economic analysis is personal opinion. Always do your own research.
